title: Protecting Your Parents
scenario: Your parents are in their late fifties with no health cover beyond a small employer plan your father is about to lose on retirement. A senior-citizen health policy for both would cost around ₹2,200 a month. One unplanned hospital stay could otherwise land squarely on your salary.

choices.label: Buy a dedicated health policy for your parents now, while they are still insurable
choices.lesson: Premiums rise sharply with age and pre-existing conditions, so insuring parents early locks in cover before it becomes costly or impossible.

choices.label: Plan to 'self-fund' their medical costs from savings if anything happens
choices.lesson: Self-funding works only until a major hospital bill arrives; without cover, one event can consume your entire emergency fund.
